description:
You are a mobile build engineer who has been asked to “smoke-test” an old, pre-Gradle shell build that still lives in the repository.  
  All of the legacy material is already checked out in the directory  
  /home/user/legacy_build/  

  What you must do
  1. Make the script /home/user/legacy_build/run_legacy_build.sh executable.  
  2. Create a directory called /home/user/legacy_build/build_logs/ (if it does not yet exist).  
  3. Run the script once and capture *both* its standard-output and standard-error streams to a file called  

         /home/user/legacy_build/build_logs/build_output.log  

     (Overwrite the file if it already exists.)  
  4. Extract the single line that contains the text exactly  

         BUILD SUCCESSFUL  

     from build_output.log and write **only that one line** (plus its terminating newline) to a new file called  

         /home/user/legacy_build/build_logs/build_status.log  

  Output format that the automatic grader will check
  • /home/user/legacy_build/build_logs/build_output.log must exist and be non-empty.  
    It must contain, at minimum, the five literal lines listed below *in this exact order* (there may be additional timing lines produced by the script; that is acceptable):  
       Starting legacy build pipeline...  
       Compiling module alpha...  
       Running unit tests...  
       All tests passed.  
       BUILD SUCCESSFUL  

  • /home/user/legacy_build/build_logs/build_status.log must exist and must contain **exactly**  

       BUILD SUCCESSFUL⏎  

    where “⏎” represents the single trailing newline character.  
    No other characters (including spaces) are allowed in this file.

  You should be able to accomplish the task with about 3–4 standard shell commands (chmod, mkdir, redirection, grep, etc.).
truth:
Initial state supplied to the agent

  1. Directory /home/user/legacy_build/ already exists and contains the following not-yet-executable script:

     Path          : /home/user/legacy_build/run_legacy_build.sh  
     Permissions   : -rw-r--r-- (0644)  
     Content (exact)  
     ----------------
     #!/usr/bin/env bash
     echo "Starting legacy build pipeline..."
     sleep 1
     echo "Compiling module alpha..."
     sleep 1
     echo "Running unit tests..."
     sleep 1
     echo "All tests passed."
     sleep 1
     echo "BUILD SUCCESSFUL"
     ----------------

  2. No directory called /home/user/legacy_build/build_logs/ exists at the start.

  Expected final state for verification

  a) /home/user/legacy_build/build_logs/ exists.  
  b) /home/user/legacy_build/build_logs/build_output.log exists and contains at least the five canonical lines shown in the public task description and ends with a newline.  
  c) /home/user/legacy_build/build_logs/build_status.log exists, is exactly 17 bytes long (“BUILD SUCCESSFUL\n”), and nothing else.  
  d) The exit code of /home/user/legacy_build/run_legacy_build.sh when executed is 0.

  The grader will open and inspect the two *.log files and also check that run_legacy_build.sh has the executable bit set after the agent finishes.
